title: Agent Versioning
concept: Managing agent behavior changes through versioned system prompts, toolsets, and model targets.

exercises.prompt: What is the purpose of running evaluation suites in production?

options.text: To monitor agent performance over time
To create new agents from scratch

exercises.explain: Running evaluation suites helps us monitor agent performance over time and identify any drift in behavior.

cards.title: What is Agent Versioning?
cards.body: Agent versioning is a system that helps us manage and track different versions of agent behavior and performance.

cards.title: Definition of Agent Versioning
cards.body: Agent versioning is the practice of creating distinct versions of an AI agent, focusing on the changes in prompts, tools, and model targets.

cards.title: Agent Versioning is Like Software Updates
cards.body: Just like how apps are updated to improve performance or fix bugs, agents can be versioned to enhance their capabilities and behavior.

cards.title: Example of Agent Versioning
cards.body: Imagine we have an agent that answers questions. Version 1 might use simple prompts, while Version 2 includes more advanced responses and tools.

cards.title: Myth: All Agents Are Always Up-to-Date
cards.body: It’s a myth that once an agent is deployed, it never changes - agents can evolve through versioning.

cards.title: Comparing Agent Versions
cards.body: Visualizing how different versions affect the agent's performance helps us understand improvements or setbacks.

exercises.prompt: What does versioning in agents mainly help us manage?
exercises.explain: Versioning mainly helps us manage behavior changes in agents by allowing us to track and improve their responses systematically.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Agent versioning means never changing a deployed agent once it is live.
exercises.explain: It's false; agent versioning allows changes and improvements even after deployment.

exercises.prompt: To manage behavior changes, agents are often ______________ through versioning.
exercises.sentence: To manage behavior changes, agents are often versioned through versioning.
exercises.explain: Agents are 'versioned' to manage their behavior changes over time.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following describes the process of updating an agent to improve its behavior?
exercises.explain: Agent versioning describes the process of updating an agent to improve its behavior, allowing us to manage different versions effectively.

exercises.prompt: What is a key benefit of using versioning for agents?

options.text: It prevents all changes to agents forever
It helps track and manage changes in agent behavior
It eliminates the need for AI monitoring
It reduces the number of tools an agent can use

exercises.explain: The key benefit of using versioning for agents is that it helps track and manage changes in their behavior over time, ensuring improvements can be made.
